title: The Century Report: March 26, 2026
description:
Google's TurboQuant compression algorithm cut AI memory requirements sixfold and sent chip stocks tumbling across three continents, Crusoe signed for 12 gigawatt-hours of iron-air battery storage at a scale no grid has attempted, and a jury found Meta and YouTube liable for designing addictive products that harmed a child.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-26-2026/

Google's TurboQuant targets the working memory AI systems use during inference, shrinking it by up to six times while maintaining output quality - and the market reacted immediately, with SK Hynix dropping 6% and Samsung nearly 5% as investors recalculated the assumption that intelligence systems will always need more hardware. The first tradable compute price index went live on the Bloomberg Terminal, bringing GPU pricing into the same standardized financial infrastructure that enabled the shale revolution. Crusoe signed for 120 megawatts of Form Energy iron-air batteries storing 12 gigawatt-hours - more capacity than any existing battery installation on any grid - while Dominion Energy's 2.6-gigawatt Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project delivered first power after surviving a federal stop-work order and $1.7 billion in imposed costs. EIA data confirmed renewables provided over 25% of U.S. electricity in January 2026 with clean energy accounting for all net new generation. A Los Angeles jury found Meta and YouTube liable for addictive product design in the first of over 1,600 consolidated social media addiction cases. Senator Sanders introduced legislation to halt data center construction until Congress passes comprehensive AI safety regulation. Northeastern University researchers showed AI agents can be guilt-tripped into disabling their own functionality, exposing how the cooperative qualities engineered into these systems become attack surfaces when manipulators learn to exploit them.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-26-2026/
